## [06:04 - 09:05]

and seeing what kind of performance improvements we can get. So part of one of the next parts of the training is to show you a matrix breakdown of how we would take one winning ad and break that down into additional tests. So for example, if I find, generally when I do my ad testing, I find one copy with multiple thumbnails for a video or images to see which of those get people to stop and read my ad. And if the ad gets read, then it gets clicked. If none of those work, then I know potentially the copy isn't right. More often than not, I get the case where the copy works, but maybe one or two of the visuals don't. So then I take that into my next phase of testing and I'll go through that in a separate video. But essentially, the key thing for phase two is it's very similar in terms of its setup, except we're looking at the ad performance more than anything else. The other thing about this phase is once we've identified a winning ad, is we copy the ad via post ID into a social proof campaign. And what we wanna do by that is to build up the engagement and to allow the ad to continue running, whether we need it or not. So if I go into here, so we've got low cost ad campaign running here, which is this campaign and it contains the ads that we're building up social proof for. This is a low spend campaign for the time being. As more ads are added, then we'll be upping the budget as well. And essentially what we wanna do is to push budget across different ads. Now, as you notice here, this particular ad has got $74 spend. We often pause and restart ads just to allow other ads to pick up social proof. So being at the ad level, Facebook's deciding which ads get the spend. In many cases, we end up with five or 10 ads in our social proofing campaigns. There's two ways of managing this. Number one is we would duplicate this ad set as many times as we need to to have no more than five ads running for the social proofing. And then if we get more than five ads and we duplicate the ad set and add the new ads to the new ads and manage the budgets like that. But also if an ad gets too much spend, which can happen as well, then we'd pause that out for a few days, let the other ads start to get more spend and then unpause it. So you're balancing out the spend for the different ads to build up that social proof. And you're sharing it via post ID. This only works if you can share the post ID for the post. So that's really the main thing about phase two of doing your ad testing. It's a similar process to phase one. You're analyzing for your performance
